message:
test(fcp-core): pin TaintLevel 3-variant ordering invariants (flywheel_connectors-8lxv3)

Bead asks for CompactionLevel ordering (None < Compact < Aggressive).
fcp-core has no type literally named CompactionLevel and no enum
Compact / Aggressive — the closest 3-variant ordered enum is
TaintLevel (capability.rs:2766) with variants
Untainted < Tainted < HighlyTainted. Inline test
taint_level_ordering covers two strict-< assertions but nothing
else about the ordering contract.

Coverage:
- Strict total order across all 3 variants (each pair, both directions)
- cmp() returns documented Ordering for every pair (Equal/Less/Greater)
- Default is the lowest variant (Untainted, the bead's "None" analogue)
- Discriminant order matches Ord: Untainted=0 < Tainted=1 <
  HighlyTainted=2; sort by `as u8` matches sort by Ord
- max / min / sort behave consistently with the documented order
- Order properties: reflexivity, antisymmetry, transitivity
- is_tainted predicate truth table via Provenance: false on
  Untainted, true on Tainted and HighlyTainted
- Per-variant JSON tag form pinned (PascalCase since no
  rename_all is set on the enum) and round-trip preserves;
  snake_case + unknown variants rejected
- CBOR round-trip preserves variant identity per level and
  preserves relative ordering across all 3 levels
- Copy semantics preserve equality + Ordering::Equal across copies
- Distinctness via `as u8` discriminants (TaintLevel doesn't
  derive Hash so this is the observable substitute)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
